3	
4	import {

6	  OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput,
7	  ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS,
8	  OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput,
9	  OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput,
10	  OrchestrationReplayEventsInput,
11	} from "./orchestration";
12	import {
13	  GitCheckoutInput,
14	  GitCreateBranchInput,
15	  GitCreateWorktreeInput,
16	  GitInitInput,
17	  GitListBranchesInput,

31	import { KeybindingRule } from "./keybindings";
32	import { ProjectSearchEntriesInput, ProjectWriteFileInput } from "./project";
33	import { OpenInEditorInput } from "./editor";
34	
35	// ── WebSocket RPC Method Names ───────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	export const WS_METHODS = {
38	  // Project registry methods
39	  projectsList: "projects.list",
40	  projectsAdd: "projects.add",
41	  projectsRemove: "projects.remove",
42	  projectsSearchEntries: "projects.searchEntries",
43	  projectsWriteFile: "projects.writeFile",
44	
45	  // Shell methods
46	  shellOpenInEditor: "shell.openInEditor",
47	
48	  // Git methods
49	  gitPull: "git.pull",
50	  gitStatus: "git.status",
51	  gitRunStackedAction: "git.runStackedAction",
52	  gitListBranches: "git.listBranches",
53	  gitCreateWorktree: "git.createWorktree",
54	  gitRemoveWorktree: "git.removeWorktree",
55	  gitCreateBranch: "git.createBranch",
56	  gitCheckout: "git.checkout",
57	  gitInit: "git.init",
58	
59	  // Terminal methods
60	  terminalOpen: "terminal.open",
61	  terminalWrite: "terminal.write",
62	  terminalResize: "terminal.resize",
63	  terminalClear: "terminal.clear",
64	  terminalRestart: "terminal.restart",
65	  terminalClose: "terminal.close",
66	
67	  // Server meta
68	  serverGetConfig: "server.getConfig",
69	  serverUpsertKeybinding: "server.upsertKeybinding",
70	} as const;
71	
72	// ── Push Event Channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	export const WS_CHANNELS = {
75	  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
76	  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
77	  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
78	} as const;
79	
80	// -- Tagged Union of all request body schemas ─────────────────────────
81	
82	const tagRequestBody = <const Tag extends string, const Fields extends Schema.Struct.Fields>(
83	  tag: Tag,
84	  schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>,
85	) =>
86	  schema.mapFields(
87	    Struct.assign({ _tag: Schema.tag(tag) }),
88	    // PreserveChecks is safe here. No existing schema should have checks depending on the tag
89	    { unsafePreserveChecks: true },
90	  );
91	
92	const WebSocketRequestBody = Schema.Union([
93	  // Orchestration methods
94	  tagRequestBody(
95	    ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.dispatchCommand,
96	    Schema.Struct({ command: ClientOrchestrationCommand }),
97	  ),
98	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getSnapshot, OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput),
99	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getTurnDiff, OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput),
100	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getFullThreadDiff, OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput),
101	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.replayEvents, OrchestrationReplayEventsInput),
102	
103	  // Project Search
104	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsSearchEntries, ProjectSearchEntriesInput),
105	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsWriteFile, ProjectWriteFileInput),
106	
107	  // Shell methods
108	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.shellOpenInEditor, OpenInEditorInput),
109	
110	  // Git methods
111	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitPull, GitPullInput),
112	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitStatus, GitStatusInput),
113	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRunStackedAction, GitRunStackedActionInput),
114	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitListBranches, GitListBranchesInput),
115	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateWorktree, GitCreateWorktreeInput),
116	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRemoveWorktree, GitRemoveWorktreeInput),
117	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateBranch, GitCreateBranchInput),
118	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCheckout, GitCheckoutInput),
119	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitInit, GitInitInput),
120	
121	  // Terminal methods
122	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalOpen, TerminalOpenInput),
123	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalWrite, TerminalWriteInput),
124	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalResize, TerminalResizeInput),
125	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClear, TerminalClearInput),
126	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalRestart, TerminalRestartInput),
127	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClose, TerminalCloseInput),
128	
129	  // Server meta
130	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverGetConfig, Schema.Struct({})),
131	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverUpsertKeybinding, KeybindingRule),
132	]);
133	
134	export const WebSocketRequest = Schema.Struct({
135	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
136	  body: WebSocketRequestBody,
137	});
138	export type WebSocketRequest = typeof WebSocketRequest.Type;
139	
140	export const WebSocketResponse = Schema.Struct({
141	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
142	  result: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
143	  error: Schema.optional(
144	    Schema.Struct({
145	      message: Schema.String,
146	    }),
147	  ),
148	});
149	export type WebSocketResponse = typeof WebSocketResponse.Type;
150

41	  projectsRemove: "projects.remove",

43	  projectsWriteFile: "projects.writeFile",
44	
45	  // Shell methods
46	  shellOpenInEditor: "shell.openInEditor",
47	
48	  // Git methods
49	  gitPull: "git.pull",
50	  gitStatus: "git.status",
51	  gitRunStackedAction: "git.runStackedAction",
52	  gitListBranches: "git.listBranches",
53	  gitCreateWorktree: "git.createWorktree",
54	  gitRemoveWorktree: "git.removeWorktree",
55	  gitCreateBranch: "git.createBranch",
56	  gitCheckout: "git.checkout",
57	  gitInit: "git.init",
58	
59	  // Terminal methods
60	  terminalOpen: "terminal.open",
61	  terminalWrite: "terminal.write",
62	  terminalResize: "terminal.resize",
63	  terminalClear: "terminal.clear",
64	  terminalRestart: "terminal.restart",
65	  terminalClose: "terminal.close",
66	
67	  // Server meta
68	  serverGetConfig: "server.getConfig",
69	  serverUpsertKeybinding: "server.upsertKeybinding",
70	} as const;
71	
72	// ── Push Event Channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	export const WS_CHANNELS = {
75	  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
76	  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
77	  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
78	} as const;
79	
80	// -- Tagged Union of all request body schemas ─────────────────────────
81	
82	const tagRequestBody = <const Tag extends string, const Fields extends Schema.Struct.Fields>(
83	  tag: Tag,
84	  schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>,
85	) =>
86	  schema.mapFields(
87	    Struct.assign({ _tag: Schema.tag(tag) }),
88	    // PreserveChecks is safe here. No existing schema should have checks depending on the tag
89	    { unsafePreserveChecks: true },
90	  );
91	
92	const WebSocketRequestBody = Schema.Union([
93	  // Orchestration methods
94	  tagRequestBody(
95	    ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.dispatchCommand,
96	    Schema.Struct({ command: ClientOrchestrationCommand }),
97	  ),
98	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getSnapshot, OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput),
99	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getTurnDiff, OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput),
100	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getFullThreadDiff, OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput),
101	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.replayEvents, OrchestrationReplayEventsInput),
102	
103	  // Project Search
104	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsSearchEntries, ProjectSearchEntriesInput),
105	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsWriteFile, ProjectWriteFileInput),
106	
107	  // Shell methods
108	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.shellOpenInEditor, OpenInEditorInput),
109	
110	  // Git methods
111	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitPull, GitPullInput),
112	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitStatus, GitStatusInput),
113	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRunStackedAction, GitRunStackedActionInput),
114	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitListBranches, GitListBranchesInput),
115	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateWorktree, GitCreateWorktreeInput),
116	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRemoveWorktree, GitRemoveWorktreeInput),
117	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateBranch, GitCreateBranchInput),
118	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCheckout, GitCheckoutInput),
119	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitInit, GitInitInput),
120	
121	  // Terminal methods
122	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalOpen, TerminalOpenInput),
123	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalWrite, TerminalWriteInput),
124	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalResize, TerminalResizeInput),
125	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClear, TerminalClearInput),
126	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalRestart, TerminalRestartInput),
127	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClose, TerminalCloseInput),
128	
129	  // Server meta
130	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverGetConfig, Schema.Struct({})),
131	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverUpsertKeybinding, KeybindingRule),
132	]);
133	
134	export const WebSocketRequest = Schema.Struct({
135	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
136	  body: WebSocketRequestBody,
137	});
138	export type WebSocketRequest = typeof WebSocketRequest.Type;
139	
140	export const WebSocketResponse = Schema.Struct({
141	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
142	  result: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
143	  error: Schema.optional(
144	    Schema.Struct({
145	      message: Schema.String,
146	    }),
147	  ),
148	});
149	export type WebSocketResponse = typeof WebSocketResponse.Type;
150	
151	export const WsPush = Schema.Struct({
152	  type: Schema.Literal("push"),
153	  channel: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
154	  data: Schema.Unknown,
155	});
156	export type WsPush = typeof WsPush.Type;
157	
158	// ── Union of all server → client messages ─────────────────────────────
159	
160	export const WsResponse = Schema.Union([WebSocketResponse, WsPush]);
161	export type WsResponse = typeof WsResponse.Type;
162	
163	// ── Server welcome payload ───────────────────────────────────────────
164	
165	export const WsWelcomePayload = Schema.Struct({
166	  cwd: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
167	  projectName: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
168	  bootstrapProjectId: Schema.optional(ProjectId),
169	  bootstrapThreadId: Schema.optional(ThreadId),
170	});
171	export type WsWelcomePayload = typeof WsWelcomePayload.Type;


---

### 2. MAIN ENTRY POINT FILES

**`/apps/web/src/main.tsx`:**
- React 19 entry point using ReactDOM.createRoot
- Routes setup via TanStack Router with hash history (Electron) or browser history
- Imports Tailwind CSS and custom index.css
- Wraps app in React.StrictMode and RouterProvider

**`/apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`:**
- Root route component with context providers
- Wraps in `ToastProvider` and `AnchoredToastProvider` (for toast notifications)
- Contains `EventRouter` component that syncs server state to Zustand store
- Uses `useQueryClient()` for React Query management
- `DesktopProjectBootstrap` component for Electron hydration
- Renders Outlet for nested routes
- Error boundary with custom error display UI

---

### 3. REACT CONTEXT & PROVIDER SETUP

**Context/Provider Hierarchy:**
1. **RouterProvider** (TanStack Router) - Top level
2. **QueryClientProvider** (React Query) - Data fetching
3. **StoreProvider** - Wraps app with Zustand store
4. **ToastProvider + AnchoredToastProvider** - Toast notifications
5. **SidebarProvider** - Sidebar state (in _chat.tsx route)
6. **DiffWorkerPoolProvider** - Web worker pool for diffs

**All providers are set up in `router.ts`:**
```typescript
export function getRouter(history: RouterHistory) {
  const queryClient = new QueryClient();
  return createRouter({
    routeTree,
    history,
    context: { queryClient },
    Wrap: ({ children }) =>
      createElement(
        QueryClientProvider,
        { client: queryClient },
        createElement(StoreProvider, null, children),
      ),
  });
}
```

---

### 4. LOCAL STORAGE PATTERNS

The app uses **localStorage** extensively via Zustand's persist middleware:

**Storage Keys Used:**
- `t3code:theme` - Theme preference (light/dark/system)
- `t3code:renderer-state:v8` - App state (expanded projects)
- `t3code:terminal-state:v1` - Terminal UI state per thread
- `t3code:composer-drafts:v1` - Composer draft messages/state
- `t3code:app-settings:v1` - App settings (models, codex paths, etc.)

**Storage Implementation Pattern:**

1. **useTheme Hook** (`hooks/useTheme.ts`):
   - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external store subscription
   - Manually manages localStorage with listeners
   - Stores: `localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)` / `localStorage.setItem()`
   - No abstraction layer - direct localStorage access

2. **Zustand Stores** with persist middleware:
   ```typescript
   create<StoreName>()(
     persist(
       (set) => ({ /* store impl */ }),
       {
         name: 'storage-key',
         version: 1,
         storage: createJSONStorage(() => localStorage),
         partialize: (state) => ({ /* only persist these */ }),
       }
     )
   )
   ```

**Stores using localStorage:**
- `store.ts` - useStore (app state)
- `terminalStateStore.ts` - useTerminalStateStore (terminal UI state)
- `composerDraftStore.ts` - useComposerDraftStore (draft messages)

**Non-persisted stores:**
- `appSettings.ts` - Custom implementation without Zustand persist

---

### 5. CSS VARIABLES & THEME SYSTEM

**Main CSS File:** `/apps/web/src/index.css`

**CSS Custom Properties (Variables) Defined in `:root`:**

**Light Theme (default):**
```css
--background: var(--color-white)
--foreground: var(--color-neutral-800)
--card: var(--color-white)
--card-foreground: var(--color-neutral-800)
--popover: var(--color-white)
--popover-foreground: var(--color-neutral-800)
--primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)
--primary-foreground: var(--color-white)
--secondary: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)
--muted: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)
--muted-foreground: color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-neutral-500) 90%, var(--color-black))
--accent: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 4%)
--destructive: var(--color-red-500)
--destructive-foreground: var(--color-red-700)
--border: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 8%)
--input: --alpha(var(--color-black) / 10%)
--ring: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)
--info: var(--color-blue-500)
--success: var(--color-emerald-500)
--warning: var(--color-amber-500)
--radius: 0.625rem
```

**Dark Theme (`:root.dark` or `:root.dark *`):**
- Automatically switched via `.dark` class on `<html>` element
- Darker background, lighter foreground
- Adjusted opacity values for borders/inputs

**Tailwind Integration:**
- Uses `@import "tailwindcss"` at top of index.css
- Custom `@theme inline` block maps Tailwind colors to CSS variables
- Tailwind 4.0 with `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin

**Theme Application:**
- `useTheme()` hook toggles `.dark` class on `document.documentElement`
- Suppresses transitions during theme changes with `.no-transitions` class
- System preference listening via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
- Cross-tab sync via localStorage `storage` event

---

### 6. EXISTING UI COMPONENTS & PATTERNS

**Selector/Dropdown Components:**

1. **Select** (`components/ui/select.tsx`):
   - Based on `@base-ui/react/select` primitives
   - Components: `SelectTrigger`, `SelectValue`, `SelectPopup`, `SelectItem`, `SelectSeparator`, `SelectGroup`, `SelectGroupLabel`
   - Uses CVA (class-variance-authority) for variants: `default` & `ghost`
   - Sizes: `default`, `lg`, `sm`
   - Styled with Tailwind + CSS variables

2. **Combobox** (`components/ui/combobox.tsx`):
   - Based on `@base-ui/react/combobox` primitives
   - Searchable dropdown with optional multi-select
   - Components: `Combobox`, `ComboboxInput`, `ComboboxPopup`, `ComboboxItem`, `ComboboxChips`, `ComboboxChip`
   - Uses React Context for chips management
   - Integrates with `ScrollArea` component
   - Supports addons (prefix icons) and clear button

3. **Button** (`components/ui/button.tsx`):
   - Based on `@base-ui/react/` (headless)
   - CVA variants: `default`, `destructive`, `ghost`, `link`, `outline`, `secondary`, `destructive-outline`
   - Sizes: `default`, `icon`, `icon-lg`, `icon-sm`, `icon-xs`, `icon-xl`, `lg`, `sm`, `xl`, `xs`
   - Uses `useRender` hook from @base-ui/react for flexible rendering

4. **Input** (`components/ui/input.tsx`):
   - Standard text input with Tailwind styling
   - Supports size variants and disabled state

5. **Other Components**:
   - Switch, Checkbox, Radio Group, Toggle, Toggle Group
   - Dialog, Sheet, Popover, Menu
   - Badge, Card, Alert, Skeleton, Spinner
   - Toast with custom logic
   - Sidebar with collapse functionality

**All UI components:**
- Use `cn()` utility from lib/utils.ts (tailwind-merge + clsx)
- Use Lucide React icons
- Support data-slot attributes for styling/selection
- Follow accessibility patterns from base-ui

---

### 7. APP STRUCTURE & ROUTING

**Router Setup:**
- TanStack Router v1.160+ with file-based routing
- Routes are in `src/routes/` directory
- Generated route tree: `routeTree.gen.ts` (auto-generated)

**Route Structure:**
```
__root.tsx (root layout with providers)
├── _chat.tsx (chat layout with sidebar)
│   ├── _chat.index.tsx (home)
│   ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx (thread detail)
│   └── _chat.settings.tsx (settings)
```

**Navigation:**
- Using TanStack Router's `useNavigate()` hook
- Hash-based history for Electron (createHashHistory)
- Browser history for web (createBrowserHistory)

**URL Scheme:**
- Chat: `/#/` or `/`
- Thread: `/#/$threadId` or `/$threadId`
- Settings: `/#/settings` or `/settings`

---

### 8. STATE MANAGEMENT & STORES

**Zustand Stores (React hooks-based):**

1. **`useStore`** (`store.ts`):
   - Main app state: projects, threads, hydration status
   - Persisted to localStorage as `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - Actions: `syncServerReadModel()`, `markThreadVisited()`, `toggleProject()`, `setError()`, etc.
   - Persists only expanded project CWDs

2. **`useTerminalStateStore`** (`terminalStateStore.ts`):
   - Per-thread terminal UI state: open/closed, height, active terminal, groups
   - Persisted to localStorage
   - Actions: `setTerminalOpen()`, `setTerminalHeight()`, `splitTerminal()`, `newTerminal()`, etc.
   - Complex normalization logic to maintain consistency

3. **`useComposerDraftStore`** (`composerDraftStore.ts`):
   - Per-thread draft message state
   - Persisted to localStorage as `t3code:composer-drafts:v1`
   - Handles image attachments with file data
   - Actions for setting drafts, attachments, provider/model selection

**External Store (useTheme):**
- `useSyncExternalStore` pattern
- Manual listener management
- Syncs across tabs via storage event

**App Settings:**
- Custom store in `appSettings.ts`
- Uses Effect.js Schema for validation
- Stored as `t3code:app-settings:v1`
- Settings: customCodexModels, codexBinaryPath, codexHomePath, codexServiceTier, enableAssistantStreaming, confirmThreadDelete

---

### 9. CSS/STYLING APPROACH

**Styling Stack:**
1. **Tailwind CSS 4.0** - Utility-first framework
2. **CSS Custom Properties (CSS Variables)** - Theme management
3. **CVA (class-variance-authority)** - Component variant management
4. **tailwind-merge** - Prevent class conflicts
5. **Plain CSS** - Custom styles in index.css for specific behaviors

**Global Styles in index.css:**
- Font family: "DM Sans" with system font fallback
- Scrollbar styling (webkit)
- Terminal drawer scrollbar customization
- Drag region for Electron frameless window
- Chat markdown rendering styles (links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, tables)
- Diff panel theme bridge (matches app palette)
- Transitions suppression during theme changes

**Component Styling Pattern:**
```typescript
const componentVariants = cva(
  "base-classes with before::shadow and focus-visible:ring",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "primary-colors",
        ghost: "transparent-variant"
      },
      size: {
        default: "default-size",
        lg: "large-size"
      }
    }
  }
);

// Then in component:
className={cn(componentVariants({ variant, size }), customClassName)}
```

**Dark Mode:**
- Custom CSS variant: `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))`
- Applied via `.dark` class toggle on html element
- Tailwind respects dark mode CSS variables

---

### 10. KEY FILES TO UNDERSTAND

**Critical Entry Points:**
- `/apps/web/src/main.tsx` - React app bootstrap
- `/apps/web/src/router.ts` - Provider setup
- `/apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` - Global layout & event handling
- `/apps/web/src/index.css` - All styling & theme definitions

**State Management:**
- `/apps/web/src/store.ts` - Main app store
- `/apps/web/src/terminalStateStore.ts` - Terminal UI state
- `/apps/web/src/composerDraftStore.ts` - Draft messages
- `/apps/web/src/appSettings.ts` - Settings with validation
- `/apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` - Theme hook

**Configuration:**
- `/apps/web/package.json` - Dependencies (React 19, TanStack, Zustand, Tailwind 4, @base-ui)
- `/apps/web/vite.config.ts` - Build configuration
- `/apps/web/tsconfig.json` - TypeScript config

---

### 11. DEPENDENCIES SUMMARY

**Key Libraries:**
- **React 19.0.0** - UI framework
- **@tanstack/react-router 1.160.2** - File-based routing
- **@tanstack/react-query 5.90.0** - Server state management
- **zustand 5.0.11** - Client state management
- **tailwindcss 4.0.0** - CSS framework
- **@base-ui/react 1.2.0** - Headless component primitives
- **class-variance-authority 0.7.1** - Component variant management
- **lucide-react 0.564.0** - Icon library
- **@xterm/xterm 6.0.0** - Terminal emulator
- **@lexical/react 0.41.0** - Rich text editor

**Architecture Pattern:** Headless-first with base-ui primitives + Tailwind styling + Zustand stores + React Query for server sync.

---

This comprehensive structure allows for adding new theme options by simply managing CSS variables and the `.dark` class, while maintaining all state and storage patterns consistently across the application.

- Custom `@theme inline` block maps Tailwind colors to CSS variables
- Tailwind 4.0 with `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin

**Theme Application:**
- `useTheme()` hook toggles `.dark` class on `document.documentElement`
- Suppresses transitions during theme changes with `.no-transitions` class
- System preference listening via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
- Cross-tab sync via localStorage `storage` event

### 6. EXISTING UI COMPONENTS & PATTERNS

**Selector/Dropdown Components:**

1. **Select** (`components/ui/select.tsx`):
   - Based on `@base-ui/react/select` primitives
   - Components: `SelectTrigger`, `SelectValue`, `SelectPopup`, `SelectItem`, `SelectSeparator`, `SelectGroup`, `SelectGroupLabel`
   - Uses CVA (class-variance-authority) for variants: `default` & `ghost`
   - Sizes: `default`, `lg`, `sm`
   - Styled with Tailwind + CSS variables

2. **Combobox** (`components/ui/combobox.tsx`):
   - Based on `@base-ui/react/combobox` primitives
   - Searchable dropdown with optional multi-select
   - Components: `Combobox`, `ComboboxInput`, `ComboboxPopup`, `ComboboxItem`, `ComboboxChips`, `ComboboxChip`
   - Uses React Context for chips management
   - Integrates with `ScrollArea` component
   - Supports addons (prefix icons) and clear button

3. **Button** (`components/ui/button.tsx`):
   - Based on `@base-ui/react/` (headless)
   - CVA variants: `default`, `destructive`, `ghost`, `link`, `outline`, `secondary`, `destructive-outline`
   - Sizes: `default`, `icon`, `icon-lg`, `icon-sm`, `icon-xs`, `icon-xl`, `lg`, `sm`, `xl`, `xs`
   - Uses `useRender` hook from @base-ui/react for flexible rendering

4. **Input** (`components/ui/input.tsx`):
   - Standard text input with Tailwind styling
   - Supports size variants and disabled state

5. **Other Components**:
   - Switch, Checkbox, Radio Group, Toggle, Toggle Group
   - Dialog, Sheet, Popover, Menu
   - Badge, Card, Alert, Skeleton, Spinner
   - Toast with custom logic
   - Sidebar with collapse functionality

**All UI components:**
- Use `cn()` utility from lib/utils.ts (tailwind-merge + clsx)
- Use Lucide React icons
- Support data-slot attributes for styling/selection
- Follow accessibility patterns from base-ui

### 7. APP STRUCTURE & ROUTING

**Router Setup:**
- TanStack Router v1.160+ with file-based routing
- Routes are in `src/routes/` directory
- Generated route tree: `routeTree.gen.ts` (auto-generated)

**Route Structure:**
```
__root.tsx (root layout with providers)
├── _chat.tsx (chat layout with sidebar)
│   ├── _chat.index.tsx (home)
│   ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx (thread detail)
│   └── _chat.settings.tsx (settings)
```

**Navigation:**
- Using TanStack Router's `useNavigate()` hook
- Hash-based history for Electron (createHashHistory)
- Browser history for web (createBrowserHistory)

**URL Scheme:**
- Chat: `/#/` or `/`
- Thread: `/#/$threadId` or `/$threadId`
- Settings: `/#/settings` or `/settings`

---

### 8. STATE MANAGEMENT & STORES

**Zustand Stores (React hooks-based):**

1. **`useStore`** (`store.ts`):
   - Main app state: projects, threads, hydration status
   - Persisted to localStorage as `t3code:renderer-state:v8`
   - Actions: `syncServerReadModel()`, `markThreadVisited()`, `toggleProject()`, `setError()`, etc.
   - Persists only expanded project CWDs

2. **`useTerminalStateStore`** (`terminalStateStore.ts`):
   - Per-thread terminal UI state: open/closed, height, active terminal, groups
   - Persisted to localStorage
   - Actions: `setTerminalOpen()`, `setTerminalHeight()`, `splitTerminal()`, `newTerminal()`, etc.
   - Complex normalization logic to maintain consistency

3. **`useComposerDraftStore`** (`composerDraftStore.ts`):
   - Per-thread draft message state
   - Persisted to localStorage as `t3code:composer-drafts:v1`
   - Handles image attachments with file data
   - Actions for setting drafts, attachments, provider/model selection

**External Store (useTheme):**
- `useSyncExternalStore` pattern
- Manual listener management
- Syncs across tabs via storage event

**App Settings:**
- Custom store in `appSettings.ts`
- Uses Effect.js Schema for validation
- Stored as `t3code:app-settings:v1`
- Settings: customCodexModels, codexBinaryPath, codexHomePath, codexServiceTier, enableAssistantStreaming, confirmThreadDelete

---

### 9. CSS/STYLING APPROACH

**Styling Stack:**
1. **Tailwind CSS 4.0** - Utility-first framework
2. **CSS Custom Properties (CSS Variables)** - Theme management
3. **CVA (class-variance-authority)** - Component variant management
4. **tailwind-merge** - Prevent class conflicts
5. **Plain CSS** - Custom styles in index.css for specific behaviors

**Global Styles in index.css:**
- Font family: "DM Sans" with system font fallback
- Scrollbar styling (webkit)
- Terminal drawer scrollbar customization
- Drag region for Electron frameless window
- Chat markdown rendering styles (links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, tables)
- Diff panel theme bridge (matches app palette)
- Transitions suppression during theme changes

**Component Styling Pattern:**
```typescript
const componentVariants = cva(
  "base-classes with before::shadow and focus-visible:ring",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "primary-colors",
        ghost: "transparent-variant"
      },
      size: {
        default: "default-size",
        lg: "large-size"
      }
    }
  }
);

// Then in component:
className={cn(componentVariants({ variant, size }), customClassName)}
```

**Dark Mode:**
- Custom CSS variant: `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))`
- Applied via `.dark` class toggle on html element
- Tailwind respects dark mode CSS variables

      - `ProjectSearchEntriesInput` - Search project files (query, limit)
      - `ProjectWriteFileInput` - Write file to project
    - Result schemas:
      - `ProjectSearchEntriesResult` - List of entries + truncated flag
      - `ProjectWriteFileResult` - Confirmation
    - `ProjectEntry` - File/directory with kind and parent path

14. **`ws.ts`** (172 lines)
    - WebSocket protocol definitions
    - `WS_METHODS` - All RPC method names:
      - Project: "projects.list", "projects.add", "projects.searchEntries", "projects.writeFile"
      - Shell: "shell.openInEditor"
      - Git: "git.pull", "git.status", "git.runStackedAction", etc.
      - Terminal: "terminal.open", "terminal.write", "terminal.resize", etc.
      - Server: "server.getConfig", "server.upsertKeybinding"
    - `WS_CHANNELS` - Push channels: "terminal.event", "server.welcome", "server.configUpdated"
    - `WebSocketRequest` - Tagged union of all possible requests
    - `WebSocketResponse` - RPC response with result or error
    - `WsPush` - Server→client push events
    - `WsWelcomePayload` - Initial connection payload

#### Test Files
- `keybindings.test.ts` - Tests for keybinding schemas
- `orchestration.test.ts` - Tests for orchestration schemas
- `provider.test.ts` - Tests for provider schemas
- `providerRuntime.test.ts` - Tests for runtime schemas
- `terminal.test.ts` - Tests for terminal schemas
- `ws.test.ts` - Tests for WebSocket schemas

---

### Subpath Export System

**packages/shared** uses direct subpath exports:
```typescript
// package.json exports
"./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" }
"./git": { "types": "./src/git.ts", "import": "./src/git.ts" }
"./logging": { "types": "./src/logging.ts", "import": "./src/logging.ts" }
"./shell": { "types": "./src/shell.ts", "import": "./src/shell.ts" }
"./Net": { "types": "./src/Net.ts", "import": "./src/Net.ts" }

// Usage
import { resolveModelSlug } from "@t3tools/shared/model";
import { sanitizeBranchFragment } from "@t3tools/shared/git";
import { RotatingFileSink } from "@t3tools/shared/logging";
import { readPathFromLoginShell } from "@t3tools/shared/shell";
import { NetService } from "@t3tools/shared/Net";
```

**packages/contracts** uses single unified export:
```typescript
// package.json exports
".": {
  "types": "./src/index.ts",
  "import": "./src/index.ts",
  "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
}

// Usage - all from root
import { 
  ThreadId, 
  ProviderKind, 
  TerminalEvent,
  GitBranch,
  KeybindingRule,
  OrchestrationSession 
} from "@t3tools/contracts";
```

---

### Key Architectural Insights

1. **Effect Library Usage**: Heavy reliance on Effect for:
   - Type-safe error handling (Effect.Effect<T, Error>)
   - Service layers with dependency injection
   - Schema validation

2. **Schema-Driven**: All types in `contracts` package are built on Effect.Schema for:
   - Runtime validation
   - Type inference
   - Codec generation

3. **Workspace Sharing**: 
   - `shared` depends on `contracts` via workspace dependency
   - `contracts` has no internal dependencies (only Effect)
   - Both packages are consumed by apps

4. **Build Strategy**:
   - `shared` - No build step, exports TypeScript directly
   - `contracts` - Built with tsdown to ESM/CJS with type declarations
   - Type resolution prioritizes source during development

5. **Configuration Standards**:
   - All extend `tsconfig.base.json`
   - Effect language service plugins in contracts for type checking
   - Consistent strict TypeScript settings

This monorepo structure supports a clear separation of concerns: raw utilities in `shared`, validated types and contracts in `contracts`, consumed by multiple consumer applications.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## T3 Code Project Structure Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the T3 Code monorepo. Here's a complete breakdown:

### Overall Monorepo Structure

**Root Directory:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775475356187-wx1rq8`

**Layout:**
```
T3 Code (Bun monorepo)
├── apps/
│   ├── desktop/     (Electron-based desktop application)
│   ├── marketing/   (Marketing website)
│   ├── server/      (Backend server)
│   └── web/         (Web application)
├── packages/
│   ├── contracts/   (Shared type contracts and schemas)
│   └── shared/      (Shared utilities and models)
├── scripts/         (Build/dev automation scripts)
├── .docs/           (Documentation)
├── docs/            (Additional docs)
├── assets/          (Static assets)
└── Root config files
```

---

### Root Configuration Files

#### `/package.json`
```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/monorepo",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": {
    "packages": ["apps/*", "packages/*", "scripts"],
    "catalog": {
      "effect": "https://pkg.pr.new/Effect-TS/effect-smol/effect@8881a9b",
      "@effect/platform-node": "...",
      "@effect/sql-sqlite-bun": "...",
      "@effect/vitest": "...",
      "@effect/language-service": "0.75.1",
      "@types/bun": "^1.3.9",
      "@types/node": "^24.10.13",
      "tsdown": "^0.20.3",
      "typescript": "^5.7.3",
      "vitest": "^4.0.0"
    }
  },
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "node scripts/dev-runner.ts dev",
    "dev:server": "...",
    "dev:web": "...",
    "dev:desktop": "...",
    "build": "turbo run build",
    "typecheck": "turbo run typecheck",
    "lint": "oxlint ...",
    "test": "turbo run test",
    "fmt": "oxfmt"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "oxfmt": "^0.35.0",
    "oxlint": "^1.50.0",
    "turbo": "^2.3.3"
  },
  "engines": {
    "bun": "^1.3.9",
    "node": "^24.13.1"
  },
  "packageManager": "bun@1.3.9"
}
```

**Key Points:**
- Uses Bun as package manager (v1.3.9)
- Bun monorepo with workspaces configuration
- Uses Turbo for task orchestration
- Effect library (functional programming) with language server support
- ESM modules only
- Oxlint and oxfmt for linting/formatting

#### `/tsconfig.base.json`
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2023",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "strict": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true,
    "noImplicitOverride": true,
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true
  }
}
```

---

### `packages/shared` - Shared Utilities Package

**Purpose:** Shared utility functions and models

#### `/packages/shared/package.json`
```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/shared",
  "version": "0.0.0-alpha.1",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "exports": {
    "./model": {
      "types": "./src/model.ts",
      "import": "./src/model.ts"
    },
    "./git": {
      "types": "./src/git.ts",
      "import": "./src/git.ts"
    },
    "./logging": {
      "types": "./src/logging.ts",
      "import": "./src/logging.ts"
    },
    "./shell": {
      "types": "./src/shell.ts",
      "import": "./src/shell.ts"
    },
    "./Net": {
      "types": "./src/Net.ts",
      "import": "./src/Net.ts"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "effect-language-service patch",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@t3tools/contracts": "workspace:*",
    "effect": "catalog:"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@effect/language-service": "catalog:",
    "@effect/vitest": "catalog:",
    "@types/node": "catalog:",
    "typescript": "catalog:",
    "vitest": "catalog:"
  }
}
```

**Subpath Exports Strategy:**
- Each subpath export maps directly to a source file
- Format: `"./subpath": { "types": "./src/file.ts", "import": "./src/file.ts" }`
- Provides direct import paths: `import { ... } from "@t3tools/shared/model"`
- No build step - TypeScript source files are exported directly
- Uses workspace resolution for `@t3tools/contracts` dependency

#### `/packages/shared/tsconfig.json`
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

#### Source Files in `/packages/shared/src`:

1. **`model.ts`** (79 lines)
   - Functions for model management and normalization
   - Imports from `@t3tools/contracts`
   - Exports:
     - `getModelOptions(provider)`
     - `getDefaultModel(provider)`
     - `normalizeModelSlug(model, provider)`
     - `resolveModelSlug(model, provider)`
     - `resolveModelSlugForProvider(provider, model)`
     - `getReasoningEffortOptions(provider)`
     - `getDefaultReasoningEffort(provider)`
     - `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`

2. **`git.ts`** (62 lines)
   - Git branch name sanitization and handling
   - Exports:
     - `sanitizeBranchFragment(raw): string` - Sanitize to valid git branch fragment
     - `sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw): string` - Sanitize to `feature/...` format
     - `resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(existingBranchNames, preferredBranch): string` - Handle conflicts with numeric suffixes

3. **`logging.ts`** (115 lines)
   - Rotating file sink implementation
   - `RotatingFileSinkOptions` interface
   - `RotatingFileSink` class - Manages rotating log files with:
     - `write(chunk)` - Append to current log file
     - Auto-rotation when max bytes exceeded
     - Pruning of overflow backups

4. **`shell.ts`** (39 lines)
   - Shell environment PATH extraction
   - Exports:
     - `extractPathFromShellOutput(output): string | null`
     - `readPathFromLoginShell(shell, execFile): string | undefined`
   - Used to capture PATH from shell login scripts

5. **`Net.ts`** (181 lines)
   - Network/port utilities using Effect library
   - `NetError` - Tagged error type
   - `NetService` - Service tag for port availability checking
   - Exports:
     - `canListenOnHost(port, host)`
     - `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port)`
     - `reserveLoopbackPort(host?)`
     - `findAvailablePort(preferred)`

---

### `packages/contracts` - Type Contracts and Schemas Package

**Purpose:** Shared type definitions and validation schemas using Effect Schema

#### `/packages/contracts/package.json`
```json
{
  "name": "@t3tools/contracts",
  "version": "0.0.8",
  "private": true,
  "files": ["dist"],
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./src/index.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./src/index.ts",
      "import": "./src/index.ts",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "tsdown src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --watch --clean",
    "build": "tsdown src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --clean",
    "prepare": "effect-language-service patch",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "effect": "catalog:"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@effect/language-service": "catalog:",
    "@effect/vitest": "catalog:",
    "tsdown": "catalog:",
    "typescript": "catalog:",
    "vitest": "catalog:"
  }
}
```

**Key Differences from shared:**
- Single unified export (root `.` export)
- Uses `tsdown` to build ESM + CJS bundles with `.d.ts` files
- Produces `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.cjs`, and `.d.ts`
- `types` points directly to source for development
- Distributed as a built package (files: ["dist"])

#### `/packages/contracts/tsconfig.json`
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "plugins": [
      {
        "name": "@effect/language-service",
        "namespaceImportPackages": ["@effect/platform-node"],
        "diagnosticSeverity": {
          "importFromBarrel": "error",
```

**packages/contracts** uses single unified export:

4. **Build Strategy**:
   - `shared` - No build step, exports TypeScript directly
   - `contracts` - Built with tsdown to ESM/CJS with type declarations
   - Type resolution prioritizes source during development

5. **Configuration Standards**:
   - All extend `tsconfig.base.json`
   - Effect language service plugins in contracts for type checking
   - Consistent strict TypeScript settings

This monorepo structure supports a clear separation of concerns: raw utilities in `shared`, validated types and contracts in `contracts`, consumed by multiple consumer applications.

**WebSocket Server Location:** `/apps/server/src/wsServer.ts`

**Communication Contracts:** `/packages/contracts/src/ws.ts`

**WebSocket RPC Methods (from contracts/src/ws.ts):**

```typescript
WS_METHODS = {
  // Project registry methods
  projectsList: "projects.list",
```

**Orchestration Methods (Codex Provider Integration):**
```typescript
ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS = {
  getSnapshot: "orchestration.getSnapshot",
```

**WebSocket Push Channels (Server → Client):**
```typescript
WS_CHANNELS = {
  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
}

ORCHESTRATION_WS_CHANNELS = {
  domainEvent: "orchestration.domainEvent",
}
```

**Request/Response Schema:**
```typescript
WebSocketRequest = {
  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
  body: WebSocketRequestBody  // Tagged union of all request types
}

WebSocketResponse = {
  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
  result?: unknown,
  error?: { message: string }
}
```

**Authentication:**
- Optional `T3CODE_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable
- Token validated on WebSocket upgrade
- Rejection with 401 Unauthorized if token mismatch

### 8. CONFIGURATION & SETTINGS PATTERNS

**Runtime Configuration Service (ServerConfig):**

Located in `/apps/server/src/config.ts`:

```typescript
interface ServerConfigShape {
  readonly mode: RuntimeMode;              // "web" | "desktop"
  readonly port: number;                   // HTTP/WS port
  readonly host: string | undefined;       // Bind address
  readonly cwd: string;                    // Working directory
  readonly keybindingsConfigPath: string;  // User keybindings.json location
  readonly stateDir: string;                // User state directory
  readonly staticDir: string | undefined;   // Web bundle location
  readonly devUrl: URL | undefined;        // Dev server proxy URL
  readonly noBrowser: boolean;              // Skip browser auto-open
  readonly authToken: string | undefined;   // WS auth token
  readonly autoBootstrapProjectFromCwd: boolean;  // Create project for cwd on startup
  readonly logWebSocketEvents: boolean;    // Verbose WS logging
}
```

**Configuration Sources (Priority Order):**
1. CLI flags (e.g., `--port 3773`)
2. Environment variables:
   - `T3CODE_MODE` - web/desktop
   - `T3CODE_PORT` - port number
   - `T3CODE_HOST` - bind address
   - `T3CODE_STATE_DIR` - state directory
   - `VITE_DEV_SERVER_URL` - dev server URL
   - `T3CODE_NO_BROWSER` - skip browser
   - `T3CODE_AUTH_TOKEN` - auth token
   - `T3CODE_AUTO_BOOTSTRAP_PROJECT_FROM_CWD` - auto-bootstrap
   - `T3CODE_LOG_WS_EVENTS` - WS event logging
3. Hardcoded defaults

**Default Values:**
- Port: `3773` (with auto-discovery for web mode)
- Host: `127.0.0.1` (desktop mode), undefined (web mode - uses all interfaces)
- Mode: `web`
- Browser auto-open: Enabled (unless desktop or `--no-browser`)
- State dir: `~/.t3code/` (platform-specific expansion)
- Auto-bootstrap: Enabled for web mode, disabled for desktop

**Keybindings Configuration:**
- Stored in `{stateDir}/keybindings.json`
- Loaded/persisted via `KeybindingsLive` service
- Updated via WebSocket `serverUpsertKeybinding` RPC method
- Validated against contracts schema

### 9. EFFECT FRAMEWORK ARCHITECTURE

**Dependency Injection Layers:**

The server uses Effect's Layer pattern for service composition. Key layers in `/apps/server/src/serverLayers.ts`:

**Provider Layer Stack:**
- `ProviderService` - Main provider orchestration
- `ProviderAdapterRegistry` - Provider backend adapters (Codex)
- `ProviderSessionDirectory` - Session storage
- `ProviderHealth` - Provider health monitoring
- Analytics logging pipelines

**Runtime Services Layer Stack:**
- `OrchestrationEngine` - Command/event coordination
- `OrchestrationProjectionPipeline` - State projection
- `OrchestrationReactor` - Event processing
- `CheckpointStore` / `CheckpointDiffQuery` - Diff/checkpoint management
- `TerminalManager` - PTY lifecycle (BunPTY/NodePTY)
- `GitManager` - Git operations
- `KeybindingsLive` - Keybinding service

**Persistence Layer:**
- SQLite via `@effect/sql-sqlite-bun`
- Event sourcing store
- Command receipt repository
- Provider session runtime repository

---

**Server Entry Points:**
- `/apps/server/src/index.ts` - Runtime layer composition
- `/apps/server/src/main.ts` - CLI program definition
- `/apps/server/src/wsServer.ts` - WebSocket server implementation

**Communication Contracts:**
- `/packages/contracts/src/index.ts` - Main export
- `/packages/contracts/src/ws.ts` - WebSocket RPC schemas
- `/packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts` - Codex provider contracts
- `/packages/contracts/src/baseSchemas.ts` - Shared ID types

**Shared Utilities:**
- `/packages/shared/src/Net.ts` - Network utilities (port finding)
- `/packages/shared/src/git.ts` - Git types
- `/packages/shared/src/model.ts` - Data models
- `/packages/shared/src/logging.ts` - Logging utilities

This is a sophisticated monorepo using Effect for functional, typed dependency injection, with WebSocket-based RPC for server-web communication and SQLite for persistence. The architecture strongly emphasizes type safety, schema validation, and composable service layers.

### 7. SERVER-WEB COMMUNICATION (WebSocket Protocol)

**WebSocket Server Location:** `/apps/server/src/wsServer.ts`

**Communication Contracts:** `/packages/contracts/src/ws.ts`

**WebSocket RPC Methods (from contracts/src/ws.ts):**

```typescript
WS_METHODS = {
  // Project registry methods
  projectsList: "projects.list",
  projectsAdd: "projects.add",
  projectsRemove: "projects.remove",
  projectsSearchEntries: "projects.searchEntries",
  projectsWriteFile: "projects.writeFile",

  // Shell methods
  shellOpenInEditor: "shell.openInEditor",

  // Git methods
  gitPull: "git.pull",
  gitStatus: "git.status",
  gitRunStackedAction: "git.runStackedAction",
  gitListBranches: "git.listBranches",
  gitCreateWorktree: "git.createWorktree",
  gitRemoveWorktree: "git.removeWorktree",
  gitCreateBranch: "git.createBranch",
  gitCheckout: "git.checkout",
  gitInit: "git.init",

  // Terminal methods
  terminalOpen: "terminal.open",
  terminalWrite: "terminal.write",
  terminalResize: "terminal.resize",
  terminalClear: "terminal.clear",
  terminalRestart: "terminal.restart",
  terminalClose: "terminal.close",

  // Server meta
  serverGetConfig: "server.getConfig",
  serverUpsertKeybinding: "server.upsertKeybinding",
}
```

**Orchestration Methods (Codex Provider Integration):**
```typescript
ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS = {
  getSnapshot: "orchestration.getSnapshot",
  dispatchCommand: "orchestration.dispatchCommand",
  getTurnDiff: "orchestration.getTurnDiff",
  getFullThreadDiff: "orchestration.getFullThreadDiff",
  replayEvents: "orchestration.replayEvents",
}
```

**WebSocket Push Channels (Server → Client):**
```typescript
WS_CHANNELS = {
  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
}

ORCHESTRATION_WS_CHANNELS = {
  domainEvent: "orchestration.domainEvent",
}
```

**Request/Response Schema:**
```typescript
WebSocketRequest = {
  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
  body: WebSocketRequestBody  // Tagged union of all request types
}

WebSocketResponse = {
  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
  result?: unknown,
  error?: { message: string }
}
```

**Authentication:**
- Optional `T3CODE_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable
- Token validated on WebSocket upgrade
- Rejection with 401 Unauthorized if token mismatch

---

### 8. CONFIGURATION & SETTINGS PATTERNS

**Runtime Configuration Service (ServerConfig):**

Located in `/apps/server/src/config.ts`:

```typescript
interface ServerConfigShape {
  readonly mode: RuntimeMode;              // "web" | "desktop"
  readonly port: number;                   // HTTP/WS port
  readonly host: string | undefined;       // Bind address
  readonly cwd: string;                    // Working directory
  readonly keybindingsConfigPath: string;  // User keybindings.json location
  readonly stateDir: string;                // User state directory
  readonly staticDir: string | undefined;   // Web bundle location
  readonly devUrl: URL | undefined;        // Dev server proxy URL
  readonly noBrowser: boolean;              // Skip browser auto-open
  readonly authToken: string | undefined;   // WS auth token
  readonly autoBootstrapProjectFromCwd: boolean;  // Create project for cwd on startup
  readonly logWebSocketEvents: boolean;    // Verbose WS logging
}
```

**Configuration Sources (Priority Order):**
1. CLI flags (e.g., `--port 3773`)
2. Environment variables:
   - `T3CODE_MODE` - web/desktop
   - `T3CODE_PORT` - port number
   - `T3CODE_HOST` - bind address
   - `T3CODE_STATE_DIR` - state directory
   - `VITE_DEV_SERVER_URL` - dev server URL
   - `T3CODE_NO_BROWSER` - skip browser
   - `T3CODE_AUTH_TOKEN` - auth token
   - `T3CODE_AUTO_BOOTSTRAP_PROJECT_FROM_CWD` - auto-bootstrap
   - `T3CODE_LOG_WS_EVENTS` - WS event logging
3. Hardcoded defaults

**Default Values:**
- Port: `3773` (with auto-discovery for web mode)
- Host: `127.0.0.1` (desktop mode), undefined (web mode - uses all interfaces)
- Mode: `web`
- Browser auto-open: Enabled (unless desktop or `--no-browser`)
- State dir: `~/.t3code/` (platform-specific expansion)
- Auto-bootstrap: Enabled for web mode, disabled for desktop

**Keybindings Configuration:**
- Stored in `{stateDir}/keybindings.json`
- Loaded/persisted via `KeybindingsLive` service
- Updated via WebSocket `serverUpsertKeybinding` RPC method
- Validated against contracts schema

---

### 9. EFFECT FRAMEWORK ARCHITECTURE

**Dependency Injection Layers:**

The server uses Effect's Layer pattern for service composition. Key layers in `/apps/server/src/serverLayers.ts`:

**Provider Layer Stack:**
- `ProviderService` - Main provider orchestration
- `ProviderAdapterRegistry` - Provider backend adapters (Codex)
- `ProviderSessionDirectory` - Session storage
- `ProviderHealth` - Provider health monitoring
- Analytics logging pipelines

**Runtime Services Layer Stack:**
- `OrchestrationEngine` - Command/event coordination
- `OrchestrationProjectionPipeline` - State projection
- `OrchestrationReactor` - Event processing
- `CheckpointStore` / `CheckpointDiffQuery` - Diff/checkpoint management
- `TerminalManager` - PTY lifecycle (BunPTY/NodePTY)
- `GitManager` - Git operations
- `KeybindingsLive` - Keybinding service

**Persistence Layer:**
- SQLite via `@effect/sql-sqlite-bun`
- Event sourcing store
- Command receipt repository
- Provider session runtime repository

---

### 10. KEY FILES REFERENCE

**Server Entry Points:**
- `/apps/server/src/index.ts` - Runtime layer composition
- `/apps/server/src/main.ts` - CLI program definition
- `/apps/server/src/wsServer.ts` - WebSocket server implementation

**Communication Contracts:**
- `/packages/contracts/src/index.ts` - Main export
- `/packages/contracts/src/ws.ts` - WebSocket RPC schemas
- `/packages/contracts/src/orchestration.ts` - Codex provider contracts
- `/packages/contracts/src/baseSchemas.ts` - Shared ID types

**Shared Utilities:**
- `/packages/shared/src/Net.ts` - Network utilities (port finding)
- `/packages/shared/src/git.ts` - Git types
- `/packages/shared/src/model.ts` - Data models
- `/packages/shared/src/logging.ts` - Logging utilities

This is a sophisticated monorepo using Effect for functional, typed dependency injection, with WebSocket-based RPC for server-web communication and SQLite for persistence. The architecture strongly emphasizes type safety, schema validation, and composable service layers.

I'm defining the theme type system with a union of three theme IDs and interfaces for color tokens and theme definitions, then setting up the built-in themes array with dark, light, and midnight variants along with a default theme constant. Now I'm creating a custom hook that manages theme state using external store synchronization, and a ThemeProvider component that reads the current theme and applies CSS custom properties to the DOM.

Actually, I'm realizing there's a circular dependency problem if ThemeProvider calls useTheme while also needing to apply the theme at module load time to prevent flash. So I'm keeping applyTheme as a standalone function that gets called at module load, within the hook's effect, and wherever else needed.

The cleaner approach is to have useTheme handle all the state management and theme application logic, while ThemeProvider acts as a semantic wrapper that uses useLayoutEffect to ensure the DOM updates before the browser paints.

Now I'm mapping out the final file structure: a shared theme configuration file with all the type definitions and built-in themes, updated package exports, and a rewritten useTheme hook that consolidates the existing theme logic. I'm creating a ThemeProvider component that applies theme colors on mount and whenever the theme changes, but I need to decide whether to export the internal `applyTheme` function or create a separate helper for the provider to use.

Actually, the cleanest approach is to move the CSS property application logic directly into `ThemeProvider` and keep `useTheme` focused purely on state management—that way the hook stays simple and the provider handles the side effects.

I'm setting up `ThemeProvider` to call `useTheme`, then use `useLayoutEffect` to apply the theme colors and dark mode class to the document root, with a transition suppression mechanism to prevent jarring visual changes.

Refining the hook implementation... Still writing the hook... Setting up the provider component...

Now I'm thinking through the Context approach—it's cleaner semantically but requires wrapping every component that uses the hook, which could be restrictive. The external store pattern is more flexible since it doesn't depend on provider placement. I'll keep the existing external store implementation and make `ThemeProvider` a lightweight wrapper that just subscribes to changes and renders children, giving us both patterns without forcing Context everywhere.

For the settings page, I'm replacing the current theme option buttons with a dedicated `ThemeSelector` component that handles the UI for switching themes.

Building the ThemeSelector component... Building the theme swatch component...

Now I'm thinking about how to integrate the `ThemeProvider` into the router setup—I need to wrap the children with it, though I should consider whether it needs to be nested inside the `QueryClientProvider` or if it can be positioned differently in the wrapper hierarchy.

Looking at the existing structure, `ThemeProvider` doesn't depend on QueryClient or Store since it only uses browser APIs like localStorage and document manipulation, so it's flexible where it goes in the tree. The key is placing it high enough to ensure the theme applies consistently across the entire app, which means adding it to `router.ts`'s `Wrap` function makes sense. I'm verifying that `Wrap` can properly use React hooks since `ThemeProvider` depends on `useTheme`, and confirming the `createElement` pattern matches the existing `StoreProvider` setup. Now I need to think through backwards compatibility—the current system stores theme values under `t3code:theme`, so the new implementation should use a different storage key to avoid conflicts. I'm writing a function that retrieves the stored theme, validating it against the new theme IDs and gracefully handling legacy values by mapping them to their modern equivalents.

I need to keep the storage key consistent since it's only used locally, and I'm thinking through the TypeScript setup in the shared package—it should stay simple without external dependencies, just pure types and constants, though I need to be mindful of the strict tsconfig settings like `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` when defining the theme types.

For accessing themes by ID, I'll use `find()` with a nullish coalescing fallback to handle the undefined case safely, and it makes sense to extract this into a helper function that returns a theme or a default fallback.

Defining the helper function... Restructuring the theme lookup to avoid the non-null assertion by destructuring the array upfront, so `DEFAULT_THEME` is guaranteed to be a `ThemeDefinition` rather than indexed access. Now for the ThemeSelector swatch, I'm picturing a compact preview that divides the space between sidebar and main content areas, with text lines and a status bar strip to give a quick visual sense of the theme's color scheme.

Now I'm mapping out the implementation: setting up the theme types and built-in definitions in a shared module, exporting them properly, then updating the hook to read from shared code and apply themes via CSS custom properties with storage migration support.

The hook will return theme state without system preference logic, maintaining cross-tab sync through storage events. Then I'm creating a provider component that initializes the theme system at the root level, and a selector component that renders the available themes as interactive buttons.

I'll update the router to wrap everything with the provider, replace the existing theme controls in the settings page with the new selector, and clean up any unused imports. The CSS and contracts stay unchanged, though I should watch for TypeScript linting issues.

Now I'm thinking through the CSS variable cascade — when we set `--primary` on the document element, it should automatically propagate to `--color-primary` since Tailwind's theme block references it, so buttons and other components using that color will pick up the theme change.

For variables like `--sidebar` and `--editor-background` that don't exist in the base CSS, they'll be undefined until the JavaScript theme system sets them via `style.setProperty` at module load, which happens early enough to avoid any visual flash. The inline styles we're applying via JavaScript will override the CSS variable definitions in the stylesheet because of specificity, so the theme values will always take precedence once the script runs.

I should also make sure the `ThemeId` type includes all three theme options: `"t3-dark"`, `"t3-light"`, and `"t3-midnight"`.

Now I'm reconsidering whether to use React Context for the `ThemeProvider`. The existing `useSyncExternalStore` approach works fine without a provider, but using Context would make the pattern more conventional. Since theme updates are rare, the performance trade-off of Context re-rendering all consumers is negligible, so I'll go with the Context approach to keep things more idiomatic.

Setting up the Context provider... I'm settling on a hybrid approach where the external store in `useTheme.ts` handles state management and cross-tab synchronization, while `ThemeProvider.tsx` acts as a simple wrapper that mounts the theme system and makes the initialization explicit. Now I'm mapping out the specific files I need to create and modify: the shared theme module, the provider and selector components, plus updates to the hook, router configuration, and package exports.

I should also handle the `color-scheme` CSS property in the `applyTheme` function so browser UI elements like scrollbars and form inputs automatically match the theme's light or dark nature.

Looking at what the task requires—background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar colors—I notice the settings page doesn't currently display a status bar component, but I can still define and apply that CSS variable through the theme system for future use. For the ThemeSelector's swatch preview, I'll show the sidebar, background, foreground text samples, and an accent indicator, with terminal and status bar as small visual strips at the bottom.

Building the swatch layout... The preview looks good with those visual elements. I'm checking how `resolvedTheme` is currently used in the settings page and thinking about whether to keep the "Active theme" display or let the new `ThemeSelector` component make it obvious which one is selected. I'll update it to show the theme name for clarity, then move forward with implementing the full solution.

For this task, I'm defining the CSS variables for editor and terminal colors in the theme system and applying them via `setProperty`, which will display in the theme swatches. Actually integrating these variables into the components themselves is a natural next step but outside the current scope.

I'm also noticing that the sidebar component likely uses the `--sidebar` CSS variable, which means if I define it in the theme system, it could immediately affect the sidebar styling without needing component changes.

Now I'm deciding where to place `ThemeProvider` in the component hierarchy—it should wrap everything at the highest level possible, so I'm adding it to the `Wrap` function in `router.ts` alongside the existing `QueryClientProvider` and `StoreProvider` to ensure it initializes early.

content.text:
Here's a summary of the complete plan:

## What will be built

**7 files total** — 3 new, 4 modified. No build config or dependency changes.

### New files
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | `ThemeId` type, `ThemeColorTokens` interface, `ThemeDefinition` interface, 3 built-in themes (`t3-dark`, `t3-light`, `t3-midnight`), `getThemeById()` helper |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | Thin root-level component that mounts the theme subscription |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | Radio-group of theme buttons, each with a 72×48px swatch showing sidebar/background/foreground/accent/status-bar colours |

### Modified files
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./theme"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **Rewrite** — replaces `"light"/"dark"/"system"` with `ThemeId`; `applyTheme` sets 8 CSS custom properties via inline styles; graceful migration of stored legacy values |
| `apps/web/src/router.ts` | Wrap `children` in `<ThemeProvider>` inside the existing `Wrap` function |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace the existing radio-button Appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />` |

### Key design decisions
- **CSS specificity trick**: inline `style.setProperty()` on `<html>` wins over all stylesheet rules, so `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` are fully theme-owned without touching `index.css`
- **`.dark` class still toggled** — the existing Tailwind dark-mode utilities (`--muted`, `--border`, `--card`, etc.) continue working unchanged
- **4 new CSS vars** (`--sidebar`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--status-bar`) are set by the theme system and ready for component adoption
- **Legacy migration** — old `"light"` maps to `"t3-light"`, old `"dark"`/`"system"` maps to `"t3-dark"` (same localStorage key)
- **Flash prevention** — `applyTheme(getStored())` runs synchronously at module load, same as today

| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | Thin root-level component that mounts the theme subscription |

| File | Change |
|---|---|

| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace the existing radio-button Appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />` |

### Key design decisions
- **CSS specificity trick**: inline `style.setProperty()` on `<html>` wins over all stylesheet rules, so `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` are fully theme-owned without touching `index.css`
- **`.dark` class still toggled** — the existing Tailwind dark-mode utilities (`--muted`, `--border`, `--card`, etc.) continue working unchanged
- **4 new CSS vars** (`--sidebar`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--status-bar`) are set by the theme system and ready for component adoption

content.text:
All 8 tasks done. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was built

### New files (3)

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`**  
Defines the entire theme contract — no runtime dependencies, pure TypeScript:
- `ThemeId` union type: `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"`
- `ThemeColorTokens` interface: 7 surface tokens (`background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `editorBackground`, `terminalBackground`, `statusBar`)
- `ThemeDefinition` interface: id, label, `isDark` flag, colors
- Three built-in themes with distinct palettes (neutral dark, clean light, deep blue-midnight)
- `getThemeById()` helper, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID` constant

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`**  
Thin root-level component that calls `useTheme()` to mount the subscription and keep the DOM in sync for the whole tree.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`**  
Radio-group of three theme buttons, each with a 72×48px swatch showing sidebar / editor / text lines / accent / status-bar colours, with an "Active" badge on the selected theme.

### Modified files (4)

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./theme"` subpath export so both `types` and `import` resolve to `src/theme.ts`.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Fully rewritten:
- Replaced `"light" | "dark" | "system"` with `ThemeId`
- `applyTheme()` now sets 8 CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring`, `--sidebar`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--status-bar`) and still toggles `.dark` so existing Tailwind utilities keep working
- Legacy migration: stored `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"/"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (same localStorage key, zero user disruption)
- Flash prevention retained: `applyTheme(getStored())` runs synchronously at module load

**`apps/web/src/router.ts`** — `ThemeProvider` wraps the outermost layer of the app tree.

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Appearance section replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`; removed `useTheme` import, `THEME_OPTIONS` constant, and `resolvedTheme` usage.

All 8 tasks done. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was built

### New files (3)

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`**  
Defines the entire theme contract — no runtime dependencies, pure TypeScript:
- `ThemeId` union type: `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"`
- `ThemeColorTokens` interface: 7 surface tokens (`background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `editorBackground`, `terminalBackground`, `statusBar`)
- `ThemeDefinition` interface: id, label, `isDark` flag, colors
- Three built-in themes with distinct palettes (neutral dark, clean light, deep blue-midnight)
- `getThemeById()` helper, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` array, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID` constant

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`**  
Thin root-level component that calls `useTheme()` to mount the subscription and keep the DOM in sync for the whole tree.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`**  
Radio-group of three theme buttons, each with a 72×48px swatch showing sidebar / editor / text lines / accent / status-bar colours, with an "Active" badge on the selected theme.

### Modified files (4)

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./theme"` subpath export so both `types` and `import` resolve to `src/theme.ts`.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Fully rewritten:
- Replaced `"light" | "dark" | "system"` with `ThemeId`
- `applyTheme()` now sets 8 CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring`, `--sidebar`, `--editor-background`, `--terminal-background`, `--status-bar`) and still toggles `.dark` so existing Tailwind utilities keep working
- Legacy migration: stored `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"/"system"` → `"t3-dark"` (same localStorage key, zero user disruption)
- Flash prevention retained: `applyTheme(getStored())` runs synchronously at module load

**`apps/web/src/router.ts`** — `ThemeProvider` wraps the outermost layer of the app tree.

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Appearance section replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`; removed `useTheme` import, `THEME_OPTIONS` constant, and `resolvedTheme` usage.
